name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
resistance_profile: Overhead position places the long head of the triceps under load at long muscle length — a lengthened-biased choice.
rom_notes: Let the elbows flex fully overhead into a deep stretch, then extend without flaring the elbows.
cues: Keep the upper arms fixed and high
Reach into a full overhead stretch
Extend fully but do not lock out aggressively
execution_steps: Set a cable to about head height and grip the rope, facing away from the stack.
Step forward into a staggered stance with the rope behind your head and upper arms raised.
Keep the upper arms fixed and high while letting the elbows flex into a deep overhead stretch.
Extend the elbows fully forward without flaring, then return under control.
good_when: You want constant cable tension on the triceps at long muscle length.
Prioritizing long-head growth with a stretch emphasis.
You want a smoother stretch stimulus than free-weight overhead work.
bad_when: Shoulder mobility prevents a comfortable overhead position.
You want to overload the shortened lockout position (use a pushdown).
common_errors: Dropping the elbows / making it a pressdown
